string 2:
Taking an army with you is proof that you have no confidence
in your own swordsmanship!

How many times must I tell you?! The Knight Corps help
enhance my beauty.

Another excuse. While we were waiting at the rendezvous
point, your corps was glitching!

How could you fall behind in swordsmanship to the knights of
your own corps?! Pathetic.

That is not true! I simply accepted their offer! I did not
ask for their help!

I could've handled it on my own without a problem! Do not
underestimate me!

You are all talk. I bet you would have lost if it were not
for everyone's help.
H-How dare you?!
{next}Calm down, already...
{next}Why are you fighting?
{end}[I should leave them alone.]

How can I calm down?! I haven't felt so insulted in a very
long time.

It's Dynasmon's fault for saying my swordsmanship is
lacking!
How unbearable. This conversation is not over yet!
...Hm? Oh, it's you again.
You've witnessed my beautiful exploits, haven't you?
Perhaps you mean "witnessed your disgrace."

I've had it with you! ...Very well. I'll prove to you what I
am capable of.
Is that so? And how are you going to do that?

There is an opponent who will more than prove my strength.
Grademon, a renowned knight.

Could you bring Grademon to me? I am not sure of its
location, however...

Since you have been all over the world, I am sure you will
be able to find Grademon!

Grademon would be the perfect opponent! Oh, where can I go
to cross swords with it?
{next}I know Grademon's location.
{next}Are you sure you can win?
{next}Maybe I should help out again...

Wonderful! So you know where Grademon is! Now I can prove my
strength and beauty!

I am not going there to fight. I am going to win. Do you
understand?

I've decided to fight on my own. I no longer need a corps.
I'll win on my own strength!

...I see. So Grademon is at the Paradise Colosseum? Thank
you for telling me!

All right, then. I'll go right away and challenge Grademon
to a duel.

Are you sure about going alone? What can you possibly do
with your waning swordsmanship?

Why not simply admit your weakness and get help before you
get seriously injured?

Dynasmon, you can stay here and wait for the results... The
news of my victory, that is!
Hey, wait...
I must go now! Farewell!
...Too late. LordKnightmon is gone.

You have been witnessing all of this. I would like to hear
your honest opinion...
Was I too hard on LordKnightmon?
{next}Yeah, maybe.
{next}Do you regret it?
{next}If you're worried, you should go after it.
I see... I knew I had gone a little overboard...
Well, a little... Very slightly.

No... I cannot do that. It would make things even more
complicated.

I simply wanted to stop this recklessness, but apparently
I ended up inflaming the situation instead.

Anyway, I'm worried that this whole affair ends
in veritable disaster.

I am sorry to ask, but could you go after them?
Someone needs to help them keep a cool head.
Why not, Grademon? Why won't you fight me?!
I have no reason to point my sword at you.

I, LordKnightmon, am challenging you to a duel. What if I
call it... a practice match?

I cannot fight someone in such a high position. There is
more than you to consider.
{next}Not fighting is chivalry, too.
{next}Why not just fight and get it over with?
{next}Is it actually a bit of a hassle?
Ah, it is you. I am glad you understand true chivalry.

Ah, it is you. Well, I heard LordKnightmon gives the
opponent a hard time, win or lose.

Well, rumor has it that LordKnightmon gives the opponent a
hard time afterward.

Argh. If it is chivalry not to fight, then I can't force you
to do so. But still...
Well... How do you think this should be settled?
{next}I've defeated Grademon before.
{next}You might as well just give up.
{next}Why not compete in a game?
What?! Tell me more about that!

No, I can't back down! If not fighting is Grademon's
chivalry, not giving up is mine!

Indeed, it's the age of e-sports. Competing with technology
sounds fine... but not now!

Now, I want our sword skills to compete! But I will
eventually fight via e-sports as well!
...What? So you've fought with Grademon before and won?
Grademon! Is that true?
Indeed.

If that's the case, then why can't you...? No. I must think
differently here.

I must respect your values, Grademon. Forcing you to fight
would not be chivalrous.
I am glad you understand.
So! I will fight YOU instead!

You have won against Grademon. That means if I defeat you, I
have also defeated Grademon!
I did not see that coming... I apologize...

I'm as ready as can be. You, too, should go prepare yourself
for this challenge!

Argh. I have lost! You were strong. But, then again,
Grademon has also lost to you.

If that's the case, then we still don't know who is
stronger—Grademon or I.
In your opinion, which was the stronger of the two?
{next}You, LordKnightmon.
{next}Grademon.
{next}You were both strong.
Is that so? I knew it! It must be true if you say so.
Oof... I see... Grademon was... stronger...

Is that kindness, or honesty? If it is your chivalry to
honor both, I will ask no more.

Thanks to you, I'm satisfied with the result. I appreciate
it.

I shall return to the rendezvous point, then. Don't worry.
I'll make peace with Dynasmon, too.
Well, then, I will see you again.

Lend me your ear. You must not tell anyone that you heard
this from me. Understood?

Grademon is at the Paradise Colosseum. I heard he is
competing in a tournament.

It cannot fight LordKnightmon as long as it wins. Someone
must defeat it. You, perhaps?

Grademon is at the Paradise Colosseum. I heard he is
competing in a tournament.

Again, do not tell anyone I told you. Let LordKnightmon get
this out of its system.
